text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Raymond Kowalski
Date of Birth: [on file]
Medical Record Number: [on file]
Accession Number: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
Ordering Physician: Urology / Urologic Oncology
Clinical Service: Urology

---

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
Transrectal ultrasound (TRUS)-guided 12-core prostate needle biopsy, cores submitted in separate labeled containers as follows:
  Containers 1–6: Left prostate cores (apex, mid, base — medial and lateral)
  Containers 7–12: Right prostate cores (apex, mid, base — medial and lateral)

CLINICAL HISTORY:
71-year-old male presenting with markedly elevated serum PSA of 187 ng/mL and an abnormal digital rectal examination (DRE) demonstrating a firm, irregular, nodular prostate gland. Transrectal ultrasound estimated prostate volume of approximately 45 cc. No prior prostate biopsy on record. Clinical concern for prostate carcinoma. Evaluate for malignancy.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and medical record number, are twelve separate containers each containing one to two tan-white, soft-to-firm cylindrical needle core biopsy fragments. Individual cores range in length from 0.9 cm to 1.8 cm and in diameter from 0.1 to 0.2 cm. All cores are submitted entirely in 1–2 cassettes per container, processed routinely, with three H&E levels cut per cassette. Representative sections are retained in paraffin block for potential ancillary studies.

Total aggregate tissue length: approximately 15.7 cm across all 12 cores.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections from cores labeled left apex, left mid-medial, left mid-lateral, left base-medial, left base-lateral, right apex, right mid-medial, right mid-lateral, right base-medial, right base-lateral, right anterior mid, and right anterior base are examined.

Malignant glands are identified in 10 of 12 submitted cores (bilateral involvement). The tumor is composed of infiltrating small-to-medium sized malignant acini with poorly formed to absent glandular lumina. The dominant pattern is poorly formed glands (Gleason pattern 4) comprising approximately 60% of the malignant component, with a significant secondary component of single cells and cords lacking glandular differentiation (Gleason pattern 5) comprising approximately 40% of the malignant component. The malignant cells demonstrate enlarged nuclei with prominent nucleoli, nuclear pleomorphism, and scant-to-moderate amounts of pale cytoplasm. Comedonecrosis is focally identified in isolated cribriform structures in one right-sided core (right base-medial).

Gleason Pattern Summary:
  - Primary pattern: 4 (poorly formed and fused glands)
  - Secondary pattern: 5 (single cells, solid sheets, comedonecrosis)
  - Gleason Score: 4 + 5 = 9
  - Grade Group: 5

Core-by-Core Involvement Summary:
  L-Apex:           Positive; 60% core involvement; Gleason 4+5
  L-Mid (medial):   Positive; 80% core involvement; Gleason 4+5
  L-Mid (lateral):  Positive; 70% core involvement; Gleason 4+5
  L-Base (medial):  Positive; 75% core involvement; Gleason 4+5
  L-Base (lateral): Positive; 55% core involvement; Gleason 4+5
  R-Apex:           Positive; 50% core involvement; Gleason 4+5
  R-Mid (medial):   Positive; 90% core involvement; Gleason 4+5 (comedonecrosis present)
  R-Mid (lateral):  Positive; 65% core involvement; Gleason 4+4
  R-Base (medial):  Positive; 85% core involvement; Gleason 4+5
  R-Base (lateral): Positive; 40% core involvement; Gleason 4+4
  R-Ant (mid):      Negative; benign prostatic tissue with benign prostatic hyperplasia
  R-Ant (base):     Negative; benign fibromuscular stroma and benign glands

Perineural invasion: Identified in 4 cores (left mid-medial, left base-medial, right mid-medial, right base-medial).

Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified.

Extracapsular extension: Not assessable on needle biopsy cores; however, extraprostatic tissue is not sampled in this specimen.

Benign findings in non-malignant cores: Benign prostatic hyperplasia (nodular glandular and stromal hyperplasia), scattered chronic inflammation.

---

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC):

The following immunohistochemical stains were performed on block [right mid-medial, accession block SP-XXXX-XXXXX-B07] and interpreted with appropriate positive and negative controls:

  PSA (prostate-specific antigen):     Positive (strong, diffuse cytoplasmic and luminal staining in malignant glands)
  NKX3.1:                              Positive (strong nuclear staining in malignant cells)
  Androgen Receptor (AR):              Strongly positive (strong nuclear staining, diffuse, estimated H-score >250)
  AMACR (P504S):                       Positive in malignant glands; absent in benign glands
  p63 / HMWCK (34BetaE12):            Absent basal cell layer in malignant glands (supports invasive carcinoma); basal cells intact in benign glands (internal positive control)
  Synaptophysin:                       Negative in malignant cells
  Chromogranin A:                      Negative in malignant cells
  CD56 (NCAM):                         Negative
  p53:                                 Scattered weak positivity; pattern consistent with wild-type p53 expression (no diffuse strong overexpression or complete loss)
  Ki-67:                               Elevated proliferation index in malignant areas (approximately 20-25% of malignant nuclei positive)

Note: Absence of neuroendocrine marker expression (synaptophysin, chromogranin A, CD56) argues against significant neuroendocrine differentiation at this time.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES — STATUS PENDING:
Molecular / genomic profiling (somatic next-generation sequencing panel, 84-gene) has been submitted on the diagnostic core block [right mid-medial, SP-XXXX-XXXXX-B07]. Results are PENDING and will be reported as an addendum upon availability.

Germline genetic testing has been ordered separately via the clinical genetics service (blood-based specimen). Results are PENDING.

FISH studies (PTEN deletion, ERG rearrangement by FISH) have NOT been ordered at this time but may be considered based on clinical context.

---

DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY:

Prostate, 12-core transrectal ultrasound-guided needle biopsy:

ADENOCARCINOMA OF THE PROSTATE
  - 10 of 12 cores positive for adenocarcinoma (bilateral)
  - Gleason Score: 9 (4+5), Grade Group 5
  - Perineural invasion: Present
  - Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
  - IHC profile consistent with prostatic acinar adenocarcinoma
  - Androgen receptor: Strongly positive
  - No neuroendocrine differentiation identified

---

PATHOLOGIST ATTESTATION:
I attest that I have personally examined the slides for this case and the above represents my diagnostic interpretation.

Attending Pathologist: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name]

Electronic signature on file.
Final report — Signed.

---

DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSTIC NOTE:
The histomorphology is diagnostic of high-grade prostatic adenocarcinoma. The combination of poorly formed glands (Gleason pattern 4) and single-cell infiltration/comedonecrosis (Gleason pattern 5) confers Gleason score 9, the highest-risk category. The extensive bilateral core involvement (10/12), presence of perineural invasion, high Ki-67 index, and markedly elevated clinical PSA (187 ng/mL) are all consistent with a high-volume, aggressive disease process. Correlation with clinical staging is strongly advised.

The IHC panel confirms prostatic origin (PSA+, NKX3.1+, AR strongly+) and supports invasive carcinoma (absent basal cell layer). The wild-type p53 IHC pattern (note: molecular correlation pending) and lack of neuroendocrine differentiation at this time are noteworthy. Clinical and molecular follow-up recommended.

---

Addendum: Pending NGS / germline results will be issued under separate accession or as a supplementary addendum to this report.
